blocks:
Ayahs

حمٓ (۱)
Ha, Meem. (1)
وَٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْمُبِينِ (۲)
By the Manifest Book! (2)
إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ فِى لَيْلَةٍ مُّبَـٰرَكَةٍ ۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا مُنذِرِينَ (۳)
We sent it down on a blessed night, and We have been warning [mankind]. (3)

Key Points

The word mubārak (مبارك) is derived from barakah (بركت), meaning enduring goodness (lasting good), and its opposite is the word shūm (ominous/ill-fated). The intent of the Blessed Night is the Night of Qadr (Power), which falls within the blessed month of Ramadan. «شهر رمضان الّذى انزل فیه القرآن»(1)

Many meanings have been suggested for the disconnected letters (al-ḥurūf al-muqaṭṭa‘ah). Perhaps the best interpretation is that the Qur’an, this great Divine miracle, is composed of these very letters, just as in Sūrah Ash-Shūrā, after the letters (حم عسق), He says: «كذلك یوحى» (Thus We reveal), meaning, We reveal in this manner. Of course, some narrations state that there is a secret and a mystery in these letters that no one knows except Allah.(2)

The word mubīn (مبین) is derived from ibānah (ابانة), meaning clear and clarifying. One of the injustices done to the Qur’an was when some scholars claimed: The Qur’an is qaṭ‘ī aṣ-ṣudūr (certain in its issuance) but ẓannī ad-dalālah (conjectural in its indication). They argued that the issuance of the Qur’an from Allah is certain, but its indications are not transparent and clear, and its concepts are conjectural. However, this is an inappropriate ijtihād (independent reasoning) in the face of the explicit text of the Qur’an, because the Qur’an has stated repeatedly that it is a light, a clarifier, and a definer of the boundary between truth and falsehood, just as He says in these verses: «الكتاب المبین» (The Clear Book). Of course, we encounter ambiguous verses (mutashābihāt) in the Qur’an that have multiple meanings, but those verses are also clarified in light of other verses.

The Qur’an is blessed in every respect: a) In terms of the One Who revealed it. «تبارك الّذى نزل الفرقان»(3) b) The Qur’an itself is blessed. «كتاب انزلناه الیك مبارك»(4) c) In terms of the time of revelation. «فى لیلة مباركة»

Question: If the revelation of the Qur’an occurred on the Night of Qadr, then what is the meaning of the revelation of verses over the 23 years of the Prophet’s mission?

Answer: The Qur’an was revealed once, entirely, upon the heart of the Prophet on the Night of Qadr, and then again, gradually, over the course of twenty-three years. This is similar to giving someone a suitcase full of clothes all at once, and then later opening it and showing him the clothes individually; or when you are gifted the collected poems of Hafez all at once, and then on every suitable occasion, a few verses from it are read to you.

In this verse, the time of the Qur’an’s revelation is considered blessed, while in other verses, the time of the descent of punishment is called ill-fated (ominous), «ایّام نَحسات»(5) Fundamentally, the blessedness or ill-fatedness of a time is either due to the bitter or sweet events that occur within it, or there is a characteristic inherent in the time itself that is hidden from us. For instance, the Qur’an praises seeking forgiveness during the pre-dawn hours (saḥar), which indicates that there is a characteristic in the element of time that we do not know.

1) Baqarah, 185. 2) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 30. 3) Furqān, 1. 4) Ṣād, 29. 5) Fuṣṣilat, 16.

Messages

1- The Qur’an was regarded as a sacred book during the time of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. (Allah swears by it) «والكتاب»

2- The Qur’an was sent down from a high station so that humanity may attain light. « انزلناه»

3- Times are different, and some possess sanctity and superiority over others. «لیلة مباركة»

4- For spiritual matters, the night is a more suitable time. «لیلة مباركة»

5- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to send warners. «كنّا منذرین»

6- For heedless people, warning (indhār) and caution are more beneficial than glad tidings (bishārah). «منذرین»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Ad-Dukhān

This Surah, which has fifty-nine verses, was revealed in Mecca and is the fifth Surah that begins with the disconnected letters (حم). The word Dukhān (دخان), meaning smoke, appears twice in the Qur’an: once in Sūrah Fuṣṣilat, relating to the beginning of the universe, and once in verse 10 of this very Surah, referring to the end of the world. Most of the content of this Surah discusses the greatness of the Qur’an and its revelation on the Night of Qadr, along with topics concerning Divine Unity (Tawḥīd), the destiny of the disbelievers, the story of Prophet Moses and the Children of Israel and Pharaoh, and the fact that the creation of the heavens and the earth was not in vain.

Ayah

فِيهَا يُفْرَقُ كُلُّ أَمْرٍ حَكِيمٍ (٤)
Every definitive matter is resolved on it, (4)

Messages

1- The Night of Decree (Laylat al-Qadr) recurs every year. (The imperfect verb, yufraqu, indicates continuity.)

2- The decrees of the Night of Decree relate to fundamental and essential matters. «كل امر حكیم» means every firm and foundational matter.

3- The Night of Decree is the night of destiny. «فیها یفرق كل امر حكیم»

Ayahs

أَمْرًا مِّنْ عِندِنَآ ۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا مُرْسِلِينَ (٥)
as an ordinance from Us. We have been sending [apostles] (5)
رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (٦)
as a mercy from your Lord—indeed He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing— (6)

Key Points

Allah is the source of mercy, "الرّحمن الرّحیم" (The Most Compassionate, The Most Merciful); the Prophet is also a source of mercy, "رحمة للعالمین" (A Mercy to the worlds) (6); and the Quran is a book of mercy, "رحمة من ربّك" (A mercy from your Lord). Regarding the greatness of the Quran, it is sufficient that: It was revealed on the blessed Night of Qadr, "فى لیلة مباركة" (In a blessed night). It is an illuminating book and a warning, "المبین" (The Clear/Manifest). It is a mercy from Allah, "رحمة من ربّك" (A mercy from your Lord).

6) Al-Anbiya, 107.

Messages

1- The revelation of the Quran is a very important matter in the sight of Allah. "امراً من عندنا" (A command from Us).

2- In the revelation of the Quran, the hands of others were in no way involved. "انّا انزلناه... امراً من عندنا" (Indeed, We revealed it... A command from Us).

3- All matters concerning the Night of Qadr are under the supervision of Allah. "امراً من عندنا" (A command from Us).

4- One of the Divine traditions (Sunan) is the sending of heavenly books and prophets. "انّا كنّا مرسلین" (Indeed, We have been senders [of messengers]).

5- The revelation of the Book alone is not sufficient; rather, it requires an explainer (a messenger). "انّا انزلناه... انّا كنّا مرسلین" (Indeed, We revealed it... Indeed, We have been senders [of messengers]).

6- The goal of revealing the Book, accompanied by the warning Prophet, is nurturing (Tarbiyah) and mercy. "رحمة من ربّك" (A mercy from your Lord).

7- We should not turn to other individuals or laws, for the true and eternal Knower and Aware is only He. The revelation of the Book, the sending of the Prophets, and the decrees of the Night of Qadr are all based on His knowledge of our needs. "انّه هو السمیع العلیم" (Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing).

Ayah

رَبِّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ ۖ إِن كُنتُم مُّوقِنِينَ (۷)
the Lord of the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, should you have conviction. (7)

Messages

1- Existence is under a single Lordship and management. «ربّك... ربّ السموات والارض و ما بینهما»

2- We should not take the Quran lightly, for its Sender is the Lord of the heavens, the earth, and all phenomena. «انا انزلناه... ربّ السموات...»

3- Only He who possesses complete knowledge of realities can manage the system well. «انه هو السمیع العلیم ربّ السموات و الارض»

4- Reflection upon the creation, administration, guidance, and perfection of existence provides the basis for attaining certainty (Yaqin). «ربّ السموات و الارض... ان كنتم موقنین»

Ayah

لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ ۖ رَبُّكُمْ وَرَبُّ ءَابَآئِكُمُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۸)
There is no god except Him: He gives life and brings death, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers. (8)

Messages

1- The object of worship (Ma'būd) must be someone who possesses the power to cause death and grant life. «لا اله الا هو یحیى و یمیت»

2- Granting life and causing death is the continuous work of Allah. (The verbs «یحیى و یمیت» are in the imperfect tense [present/future], which indicates continuity.)

3- The Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah over existence is proof that there is no deity except Him. (Yes, geographically, He holds the heavens and the earth under His Lordship. «ربّ السموات والارض و ما بینهما» And historically, He has placed you and your ancestors under His Lordship. «ربّكم و ربّ آبائكم الاوّلین»)

4- Human society is in a state of growth. «ربّكم و ربّ آبائكم الاولین»

5- Death and life are directed toward growth and perfection (Takāmul). (The phrase «یحیى و یمیت» is placed between the two occurrences of the word «ربّ» [Lord].)

6- The polytheism (Shirk) of the ancestors is also condemned, because Allah is the Lord of your ancestors as well. «ربّ آبائكم الاولین»

Ayahs

بَلْ هُمْ فِى شَكٍّ يَلْعَبُونَ (۹)
But they play around in doubt. (9)
فَٱرْتَقِبْ يَوْمَ تَأْتِى ٱلسَّمَآءُ بِدُخَانٍ مُّبِينٍ (۱٠)
So watch out for the day when the sky brings on a manifest smoke, (10)
يَغْشَى ٱلنَّاسَ ۖ هَـٰذَا عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱۱)
enveloping the people. [They will cry out:] ‘This is a painful punishment. (11)

Key Points

The word "فارتقب" (fartaqib - wait/watch) is used twice in the Quran, and both instances are in this Surah. This expression is a threat to the disbelievers and a form of consolation for the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. Although some have taken the appearance of smoke (دود) as a metaphor for famine and misery in this world, apparently the intended meaning of the smoke is the very smoke that will appear on the eve of the Day of Judgment or on the Day of Judgment itself.

Messages

1- Doubt should be the basis for investigation and the prelude to research and certainty, not a ground for negligence and idleness. Persistent doubt is subject to criticism and reproach. «فى شك یلعبون»

2- There is no ambiguity or complexity in the proofs of Monotheism (Tawhid), and the root of the disbelievers' doubt is the obstinacy that lies within themselves. «بل هم فى شك یلعبون»

3- Deniers of revelation have neither logic nor proof, and their efforts and lives are merely playful movements. «فى شك یلعبون»

4- After presenting the argument, it is time for the threat. «ربّ السموات والارض... فارتقب...»

5- The heavens will turn into smoke and gas in the future. «تأتى السماء بدخان...»

Ayahs

رَّبَّنَا ٱكْشِفْ عَنَّا ٱلْعَذَابَ إِنَّا مُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۲)
Our Lord! Remove this punishment from us. Indeed we have believed!’ (12)
أَنَّىٰ لَهُمُ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰ وَقَدْ جَآءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱۳)
What will the admonition avail them, when a manifest apostle had already come to them, (13)

Messages

1- Those who treat religion as a game will one day emerge from heedlessness and doubt and resort to pleading. «یلعبون... ربّنا اكشف»

2- Repentance offered upon witnessing the punishment is ineffective. «ربّنا... أنّى لهم الذكرى»

3- The wrath of Allah occurs only after the completion of the proof (Hujjah). «هذا عذاب الیم... و قد جاءهم رسول...»

4- Both the Book of Allah is clarifying, «والكتاب المبین»(7) and His Messenger is clarifying. «رسول مبین»(7) Ad-Dukhan, 2.

Ayahs

ثُمَّ تَوَلَّوْا۟ عَنْهُ وَقَالُوا۟ مُعَلَّمٌ مَّجْنُونٌ (۱٤)
but they turned away from him, and said, ‘A tutored madman?’ (14)
إِنَّا كَاشِفُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابِ قَلِيلًا ۚ إِنَّكُمْ عَآئِدُونَ (۱٥)
Indeed We will withdraw the punishment a little; but you will revert [to your earlier ways]. (15)
يَوْمَ نَبْطِشُ ٱلْبَطْشَةَ ٱلْكُبْرَىٰٓ إِنَّا مُنتَقِمُونَ (۱٦)
The day We shall strike with the most terrible striking, We will indeed take vengeance [on them]. (16)

Key Points

Allah's vengeance against the oppressors is not for the sake of emotional satisfaction, but rather for the sake of establishing justice. The word "بطش" (baṭsh) means seizing with majesty, awe, and severity. Some have interpreted this severe seizing as the defeat of the polytheists in the Battle of Badr.

Messages

1- The wrongdoer fabricates justifications for his actions. «تَولَّوا عنه و قالوا...»

2- Obstinacy deviates a person both in action («تَولَّوا») and in speech. («معلّم مجنون»)

3- The polytheists considered the teachings of the prophets to be derived from the teachings of the jinn. «قالوا معلّم مجنون»

4- Allah has repeatedly answered cries and removed dangers. «انّا كاشفوا العذاب»

5- Whenever the sinner witnesses Divine wrath, he says: I have believed. «انّا مؤمنون» But as soon as the danger is removed, he reverts. «انّكم عائدون»

6- Man is forgetful; only a few days after Allah's wrath has passed, he returns to wrongdoing. «قلیلاً انّكم عائدون»

7- Allah is the source of Mercy, but in certain instances, He has severe wrath. Those who take revelation lightly will be afflicted by severe wrath. «البطشة الكبرى»

8- Allah is the Protector of the prophets. He takes vengeance upon those who called the Prophet possessed by jinn and taught by others. «انّا منتقمون»

Ayahs

۞ وَلَقَدْ فَتَنَّا قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَجَآءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ كَرِيمٌ (۱۷)
Certainly We tried the people of Pharaoh before them, when a noble apostle came to them, (17)
أَنْ أَدُّوٓا۟ إِلَىَّ عِبَادَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (۱۸)
[saying,] ‘Give over the servants of Allah to me; indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you. (18)

Key Points

The word fitnah (فتنه) originally means placing gold in a furnace to purify it from impurities. It was then used for any kind of severe test that reveals the essence of a person.

Messages

1- The arrival of the prophets is a type of test for people, to determine who accepts the truth and who insists on obstinacy. «و لقد فتنّا... جاءهم رسول» (Perhaps the intent is that the liberation of people and the cessation of exploitation and humiliation is one of the Divine tests for the Pharaohites.)

2- The Divine test is a continuous and certain tradition (Sunnah). «لقد فتنّا...»

3- Historical lessons are both a consolation for the Prophet and a threat to the disbelievers. «قبلهم»

4- The one whom Allah chooses must possess the necessary merit and worthiness beforehand. «كریم»

5- Saving the oppressed (mustad'afan) from the hands of the oppressors is among the duties of the prophets. «ادّوا الىّ عباد اللّه» (Yes, the exploitation and abuse of the deprived class is the greatest social and economic corruption, and fighting against it is the primary duty of the prophets.)

6- The hands of Allah's servants must be in the hands of Allah's vicegerents (Awliya'). «ادّوا الىّ عباد اللّه»

7- People are the servants of Allah, so why does Pharaoh enslave them? «ادّوا الىّ عباد اللّه»

8- The leadership of the prophets is in line with human interests. «انّى لكم رسول أمین»

9- In certain situations, a person must state his own merits. «انّى لكم رسول أمین»

10- The secret to success in society is gaining the trust of the people. «انّى لكم رسول أمین»

11- Trustworthiness (Amanah) in receiving revelation and conveying it is one of the essential attributes of the prophets. «رسول أمین»

12- Moses' request for the release of the Children of Israel was due to his duty of prophethood, not merely out of support for his own tribe or clan. «ادّوا الىّ عباد اللّه انّى لكم رسول أمین»

Ayahs

وَأَن لَّا تَعْلُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنِّىٓ ءَاتِيكُم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ (۱۹)
Do not defy Allah. Indeed I bring you a manifest authority. (19)
وَإِنِّى عُذْتُ بِرَبِّى وَرَبِّكُمْ أَن تَرْجُمُونِ (۲٠)
I seek the protection of my Lord and your Lord, lest you should stone me. (20)
وَإِن لَّمْ تُؤْمِنُوا۟ لِى فَٱعْتَزِلُونِ (۲۱)
And if you do not believe me, keep out of my way.’ (21)
فَدَعَا رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ قَوْمٌ مُّجْرِمُونَ (۲۲)
Then he invoked his Lord, [saying,] ‘These are indeed a guilty lot.’ (22)

Messages

1- Opposition to the prophets and the oppressive exploitation of people is seeking superiority over Allah. (Moses told his opponents, who were exploiting the people, "Do not seek superiority over Allah.") «ألاّ تَعلوا على اللّه»

2- All of existence shows humility before Allah. «كلّ له قانتون»(8) It is man who is commanded not to be arrogant towards Allah. «أن لا تعلوا...»

3- For propagation, in addition to the spiritual perfections «كریم... امین» mentioned in the previous Ayah, logic and argumentation are also necessary. «بسلطان مبین» (He who possesses a miracle and clear proof gains the courage to confront Pharaohs.) «ادّوا الىّ عباد اللّه... بسلطان مبین»

4- If obstinacy exists, even the life and honor of prophets who are noble, trustworthy, and equipped with logic and argumentation are in danger. «أن ترجمون»

5- Slander and torture should not impede the work. «انّى عُذتُ... أن ترجمون» (The meaning of rajm is either slander or the throwing of stones.)

6- In the method of propagation, a reciprocal response is useful. (In response to Pharaoh who said: I am your Lord, Moses repeatedly said:) «بربّى و ربّكم»

7- Unless a person relies on Allah and considers Him His refuge, he cannot resist oppressors. «انّى عُذتُ بربّى و ربّكم»

8- Faith in the Prophet is faith in Allah. («اِن لم تؤمنوا لى» has come in place of «اِن لم تؤمنوا باللّه».)

9- Sometimes isolation and withdrawal are necessary. «فاعتزلون» (One instance of forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar) is distancing oneself from criminals.)

10- Moses, despite having a miracle, does not seek conflict with opponents; rather, his plan is to advance goals through logic, not confrontation. «فاعتزلون»

11- After passing through several stages of invitation and guidance, invoking divine punishment is permissible. «فدعا...»

12- If sin and corruption penetrate a person, the invitation of the prophets will no longer have an effect. «انّ هؤلاء قوم مجرمون»

(8) Baqarah, 116.

Ayahs

فَأَسْرِ بِعِبَادِى لَيْلًا إِنَّكُم مُّتَّبَعُونَ (۲۳)
[Allah told him,] ‘Set out by night with My servants; for you will be pursued. (23)
وَٱتْرُكِ ٱلْبَحْرَ رَهْوًا ۖ إِنَّهُمْ جُندٌ مُّغْرَقُونَ (۲٤)
Leave behind the sea unmoving; for they will be a drowned host.’ (24)
كَمْ تَرَكُوا۟ مِن جَنَّـٰتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (۲٥)
How many gardens and springs did they leave behind! (25)
وَزُرُوعٍ وَمَقَامٍ كَرِيمٍ (۲٦)
Fields and splendid places, (26)
وَنَعْمَةٍ كَانُوا۟ فِيهَا فَـٰكِهِينَ (۲۷)
and the affluence wherein they rejoiced! (27)
كَذَٰلِكَ ۖ وَأَوْرَثْنَـٰهَا قَوْمًا ءَاخَرِينَ (۲۸)
So it was; and We bequeathed them to another people. (28)

Key Points

Asr (أسر) from Isrā’ (إسراء) means migrating/traveling at night. Rahw (رَهْو) means an open, calm, and wide path. Ni‘mat (نِعمت) (with a kasra on the nūn) is the means of enjoyment, but Na‘mat (نَعمت) (with a fatḥa on the nūn) means enjoyment itself. (9) The word fākihīn (فاكهین) is derived from fākiha (فاكهة), meaning benefiting from fruit, or from fakāha (فكاهه), meaning being joyful/delighted. The Nile River is so vast that the Quran refers to it as a sea.

9) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Messages

1- Some supplications are answered immediately. «فدعا... فاسر» (The letter fā’ (ف) indicates speed.)

2- Supplication must be accompanied by effort. «فدعا... فاسر»

3- Time plays a role in planning. «لیلاً»

4- The nocturnal escape of the Children of Israel led by Moses was a form of silent struggle that Pharaoh could not tolerate, leading him to send an army in pursuit. «انكم متبعون»

5- Emigrate from the land of disbelief where you cannot achieve your goals. «فاسر»

6- Allah grants assurance and tranquility to His allies. «واترك البحر رهواً»

7- Not every open path is a sign of Divine favor; Allah left the path of the sea open so that the Pharaohites would enter and drown. «واترك البحر... انهم جند مغرقون»

8- In Pharaoh's mobilization against Moses, the wealthy, who owned gardens and luxurious homes, were also present. «كم تركوا من جنات و عیون»

9- Material resources are not salvific against the wrath of Allah. «كم تركوا...»

10- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to cause the extinction and destruction of oppressive nations and to bring another nation to power. «كذلك و اورثنا»

11- Worldly enjoyments are fleeting. «كم تركوا... نَعمة كانوا فیها فاكهین»

12- Drowning in luxury and indulgence places a person among the opponents of the truth. «كانوا فیها فاكهین»

13- Wealth is not the source of happiness; rather, sometimes it is the source of destruction. «جنّات و عیون و زروع... أورثناها قوماً آخرین»

Ayah

فَمَا بَكَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُنظَرِينَ (۲۹)
So neither the sky wept for them, nor the earth; nor were they granted any respite. (29)

Key Points

Some exegetes have said: The heavens and the earth not weeping is an allusion to the fact that the destruction of the Pharaohites did not cause any vacuum in the workings of the universe. However, the apparent meaning of the Ayah speaks of a type of feeling and consciousness for existence, which the Quran has termed as weeping.

Messages

1- The earth and the heavens possess consciousness, awareness, and feeling. «فما بكت»

2- Where the Will of Allah is present, the entire existence becomes harmonious. «السماء والارض»

3- Granting respite by Allah has conditions; sometimes the measure of sin overflows such that no respite whatsoever is granted. «و ما كانوا منظرین»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ نَجَّيْنَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ مِنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْمُهِينِ (۳٠)
Certainly We delivered the Children of Israel from the humiliating torment (30)
مِن فِرْعَوْنَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَالِيًا مِّنَ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ (۳۱)
of Pharaoh. Indeed, he was a tyrant among the transgressors. (31)
وَلَقَدِ ٱخْتَرْنَـٰهُمْ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۳۲)
Certainly We chose them knowingly above all the nations, (32)
وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُم مِّنَ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ مَا فِيهِ بَلَـٰٓؤٌا۟ مُّبِينٌ (۳۳)
and We gave them some signs in which there was a manifest test. (33)

Key Points

Considering that Allah, in another Ayah, introduced Muslims as the best of nations, "كنتم خیر امّة" (10), the intent behind the superiority of the Jews over the worlds (or mankind) in these verses is therefore superiority over the people of their own time, which they achieved due to following the Book of God, or (it refers to) superiority in certain characteristics, such as the splitting of the sea and the descent of mann and salwa (manna and quails), and so on.

(10) Aal-i-Imran, 110.

Messages

1- All historical transformations are in the hands of Allah. «و لقد نجّینا»

2- Allah comforts the Prophet and the Muslims by recalling the salvation granted to past believers. «نجّینا بنى اسرائیل»

3- Life under the rule of the tyrant (Taghut) is a humiliating torment. «نجّینا بنى اسرائیل من العذاب المهین من فرعون»

4- The secret to the destruction of people lies in their character and actions. «كان عالیا من المسرفین»

5- Allah’s plans are based on knowledge. «اخترناهم على علم»

6- Divine gifts are a means of trial. «آتیناهم... ما فیه بلاء مبین»

Ayahs

إِنَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَيَقُولُونَ (۳٤)
These ones say, (34)
إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّا مَوْتَتُنَا ٱلْأُولَىٰ وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُنشَرِينَ (۳٥)
‘It will be only our first death, and we shall not be resurrected. (35)
فَأْتُوا۟ بِـَٔابَآئِنَآ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۳٦)
Bring our fathers back [to life], if you are truthful.’ (36)
أَهُمْ خَيْرٌ أَمْ قَوْمُ تُبَّعٍ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ مُجْرِمِينَ (۳۷)
Are they better, or the people of Tubba‘, and those who were before them? We destroyed them; indeed, they were guilty. (37)

Key Points

There are various opinions regarding who the people of "Tubba'" were, including: A) The kings of Yemen, who were called Tubba' because the people were their followers (tābi'). B) Kings who succeeded one another (meaning one was a follower/successor of the other). C) Tubba' was the name of a person who was good himself, but his followers were wicked.

In the Quran, factors causing destruction and affliction by Divine wrath are enumerated, including: transgression (fisq), plotting (makr), disbelief (kufr), denial (takdhīb), oppression (ẓulm), arrogance (istikbār), rebellion (ṭughyān), and sin. As He says: «فاهلكوا بالطاغیة»(11) (So they were destroyed by the overwhelming [blast]), and or «فاخذهم اللّه بذنوبهم»(12) (So Allah seized them for their sins).

Bringing miracles is ineffective for stubborn individuals. A person who definitively says there is nothing after death, who holds such a prejudgment, and speaks with the tone of «اِن كنتم صادقین» (if you are truthful), is stubborn.

11) Al-Haqqah, 5. 12) Aal-i-Imran, 11.

Messages

1- Recounting the false beliefs and superstitions of others is permissible, provided it does not have a negative effect. «لیقولون...»

2- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ād) is the boundary between faith and polytheism (shirk). (The polytheists accepted God's Creatorship but did not accept the Day of Judgment.) «و ما نحن بمنشرین»

3- The choice of the miracle rests with Allah, not with the people. «فأتوا بأبائنا»

4- History is the best lesson for admonition. «قوم تبع»

5- Crime and sin are the means of destruction. «اهلكناهم... كانوا مجرمین»

Ayahs

وَمَا خَلَقْنَا ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا لَـٰعِبِينَ (۳۸)
We did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them for play. (38)
مَا خَلَقْنَـٰهُمَآ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۳۹)
We did not create them except with consummate wisdom; but most of them do not know. (39)

Key Points

La'b (لعب) refers to an action that is aimless, unplanned, and without result, just like children's games. These two verses serve as an introduction to the subsequent verses regarding the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah). It seems to indicate that if there were no Resurrection, creation would be aimless, because existence was created for humanity, and if humanity becomes futile (void) through death, then existence itself is also futile and vain. Therefore, He says: We did not create the heavens and the earth without purpose, but rather We had a rightful goal.

Messages

1- Existence has a purpose, so we should not be aimless. «و ما خلقنا... لاعبین»

2- The purpose of existence is nothing but the Truth (Haqq). If we do not grasp the purpose of the creation of something, the problem lies in our understanding and limitations. «الا بالحقّ»

3- A wise individual (Allah) does not await the judgment and evaluation of people in His work. «الا بالحقّ ولكن اكثر هم لا یعلمون»

4- True knowledge is realizing the truthfulness and wisdom behind divine actions. «الا بالحقّ... لا یعلمون»

Ayahs

إِنَّ يَوْمَ ٱلْفَصْلِ مِيقَـٰتُهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (٤٠)
Indeed the Day of Judgement is the tryst for them all, (40)
يَوْمَ لَا يُغْنِى مَوْلًى عَن مَّوْلًى شَيْـًٔا وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ (٤۱)
the day when a friend will not avail a friend in any way, nor will they be helped, (41)
إِلَّا مَن رَّحِمَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (٤۲)
except for him on whom Allah has mercy. Indeed He is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (42)

Key Points

Since creation has a righteous purpose, the Day of Resurrection must occur. The Wise God, Who created existence based on truth, does not abandon the crown jewel of creation to death. The word Mawla («مولى») is used to mean friend, guardian, servant, and bondman.

Messages

1- The Day of Resurrection is the day that separates truth from falsehood and the righteous from the wicked. «یوم الفصل»

2- Presence on the Day of Resurrection admits no exception. «اجمعین»

3- On the Day of Resurrection, all ties are ineffective except intercession (Shafa'ah). «لا یغنى مولى... الا من رحم اللّه»

4- On the Day of Resurrection, no kind of help whatsoever is available. «شیئاً»

5- On the Day of Resurrection, neither does an individual help another individual «مولى عن مولى», nor does a group help another group. «ولا هم ینصرون»

6- On the Day of Resurrection, although everyone is gathered and man is among the multitude, he is alone. «اجمعین... لا ینصرون»

7- Only Allah is He Whose power is accompanied by mercy; He is Mighty (Aziz) towards the disbelievers and Merciful (Rahim) towards the believers. «انه هو العزیز الرحیم»

Ayahs

إِنَّ شَجَرَتَ ٱلزَّقُّومِ (٤۳)
Indeed the tree of Zaqqum (43)
طَعَامُ ٱلْأَثِيمِ (٤٤)
will be the food of the sinful. (44)
كَٱلْمُهْلِ يَغْلِى فِى ٱلْبُطُونِ (٤٥)
Like molten copper it will boil in their bellies, (45)
كَغَلْىِ ٱلْحَمِيمِ (٤٦)
seething like boiling water. (46)
خُذُوهُ فَٱعْتِلُوهُ إِلَىٰ سَوَآءِ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (٤۷)
[The keepers of hell will be told,] ‘Seize him and drag him to the middle of hell, (47)
ثُمَّ صُبُّوا۟ فَوْقَ رَأْسِهِۦ مِنْ عَذَابِ ٱلْحَمِيمِ (٤۸)
then pour over his head boiling water as punishment, (48)
ذُقْ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْكَرِيمُ (٤۹)
[and tell them,] “Taste this! Indeed you are the [self-styled] mighty and noble! (49)
إِنَّ هَـٰذَا مَا كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَمْتَرُونَ (٥٠)
This is what you used to doubt!’ (50)

Key Points

"Zaqqūm" is a type of unpleasant food in Hell. In Surah As-Saffat, Ayah 63, it is also stated: «شجرة الزقوّم... انّها شجرة تخرج فى اصل الجحیم» (The Tree of Zaqqūm... indeed, it is a tree that emerges from the bottom of Hellfire). It is a tree or plant that grows from Hell. Yes, just as the sinful human remains in Hell forever, the tree also remains by the will of Allah. "Athīm" refers to someone who persists in sin. "Muhl" means molten or contaminated metal or copper. "Fa'talūh" means dragging accompanied by force and violence.

Messages

1- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical. (The issue of food and boiling water is a sign of the physical nature of the Resurrection.) «طعام... فى البطون»

2- Sin is the cause of divine punishment and wrath. «طعام الاثیم»

3- The punishment on the Day of Judgment is both physical and spiritual. (The physical punishment is the boiling substances, and the spiritual punishment is hearing the humiliation and mockery that they were the ones who, in the world, claimed honor and dignity only for themselves.) «ذق انّك انت العزیز الكریم»

4- The inhabitants of Hell burn from within and without. «یغلى فى البطون... صبّوا فوق رأسه من عذاب الحمیم»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ فِى مَقَامٍ أَمِينٍ (٥۱)
Indeed the Godwary will be in a safe place, (51)
فِى جَنَّـٰتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (٥۲)
amid gardens and springs, (52)
يَلْبَسُونَ مِن سُندُسٍ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٍ مُّتَقَـٰبِلِينَ (٥۳)
dressed in fine silk and brocade, sitting face to face. (53)
كَذَٰلِكَ وَزَوَّجْنَـٰهُم بِحُورٍ عِينٍ (٥٤)
So shall it be, and We shall wed them to black-eyed houris. (54)
يَدْعُونَ فِيهَا بِكُلِّ فَـٰكِهَةٍ ءَامِنِينَ (٥٥)
Secure [from any kind of harm,] there they will call for every kind of fruit [they wish]. (55)

Key Points

Sundus (سُندس) is thin silk fabric, and Istabraq (استبرق) is thick silk fabric. Hoor (حور) is the plural of Hooraa' (حوراء), referring to women who have black eyes and white bodies, and the word ‘Ayn (عین), the plural of ‘Aynaa’ (عیناء), means wide-eyed. The greatest blessing is security, because Maqam Amin (secure station) is mentioned before the other blessings. Of course, security in Paradise is comprehensive security: no fear of death, no rival, no envious person, no decline, and no extinction. Tranquility in Paradise relates both to the station itself (Maqam Amin) and to the foodstuffs. Bi-kulli fākihatin āminīn (secure with every fruit). In this world, sometimes consuming several fruits causes various illnesses.

Messages

1- The factor for benefiting from the blessings of Paradise is piety (Taqwa). Innal-Muttaqīn... (Indeed, the righteous...)

2- The fear and piety of today cause the security of tomorrow. Innal-Muttaqīna fī maqāmin amīn (Indeed, the righteous will be in a secure station).

3- Hope must be presented alongside fear. (Shajarat az-Zaqqūm... [Tree of Zaqqum] contrasted with) fī jannātin wa ‘uyūn (in Gardens and springs).

4- In Paradise, blessings are numerous and diverse. Jannātin wa ‘uyūn (Gardens and springs).

5- Abstaining from luxurious and silk clothing during the few days of this world leads to eternal enjoyment on the other day. Yalbasūna min sundusin wa istabraqin (They will wear [garments] of fine silk and thick silk).

6- Nudity is not a value anywhere, even in Paradise. Yalbasūna (They will wear).

7- Facing one another (reciprocity) in an atmosphere of piety is a value. What intensifies discord (fitnah) is the confrontation between the impious. Innal-Muttaqīn... mutaqābilīn (Indeed, the righteous... facing one another).

8- The people of Paradise have intimate and friendly gatherings. Mutaqābilīn (facing one another).

9- In Paradise, there is no turning away or turning one's back. Mutaqābilīn (facing one another).

10- Allah is the intermediary for marriage in Paradise. Zawwajnāhum bi-hūrin ‘īn (We will marry them to wide-eyed Houris).

Ayahs

لَا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا ٱلْمَوْتَ إِلَّا ٱلْمَوْتَةَ ٱلْأُولَىٰ ۖ وَوَقَىٰهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (٥٦)
Other than the first death, they will not taste death therein, and He will save them from the punishment of hell (56)
فَضْلًا مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (٥۷)
—a grace from your Lord. That is the great success. (57)

Messages

1- Protection in this world is the cause of protection in the Hereafter. Whoever protects himself in this world through piety, Allah protects him from Hellfire in the Hereafter. «انّ المتّقین... و وقاهم...»

2- Paradise is eternal, and the inhabitants of Paradise are safe from the worry of death. «لایذوقون فیها الموت...»

3- No one has a claim against Allah; everything that exists is His grace. «فضلاً من ربّك»

4- All the successes of the inhabitants of Paradise are due to Allah's favor. If it were not for the guidance, fervor, and sincerity of the Prophet, there would be no pious people (muttaqīn) to reach Paradise. «فضلاً من ربّك»

5 - True happiness is achieved through piety and salvation from Hellfire. «وقاهم عذاب الجحیم... ذلك هو الفوز العظیم»

Ayahs

فَإِنَّمَا يَسَّرْنَـٰهُ بِلِسَانِكَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (٥۸)
Indeed We have made it simple in your language, so that they may take admonition. (58)
فَٱرْتَقِبْ إِنَّهُم مُّرْتَقِبُونَ (٥۹)
So wait! They [too] are waiting. (59)

Key Points

| Allah's attention to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is so great that the names of some of His body parts are also mentioned in the Quran: - Face. "وجهك" (your face) in "قد نرى تقلّب وجهك..." (13) - Eyes. "عَینیك" (your eyes) in "لا تمدّنّ عینیك" (14) - Tongue. "بلسانك" (your tongue) in "لقد یسّرناه بلسانك" (15) - Back. "ظهرك" (your back) in "انقض ظهرك" (16) - Hand. "یدك" (your hand) in "لا تجعل یدك مغلولة" (17) - Life/Age. "لعمرك" (by your life) in "لعمرك انهم لفى سكرتهم یعمهون" (18) - Neck. "عنقك" (your neck) in "لا تجعل یدك مغلولة الى عنقك" (19) - Heart/Mind. "فؤادك" (your heart) in "لنثبّت به فؤادك" (20) - Chest. "صدرك" (your chest) in "ألم نشرح لك صدرك" (21) This Surah began by glorifying the Quran and concludes by reminding (people) of the Quran.

13) Al-Baqarah, 144. 14) Al-Hijr, 88. 15) Maryam, 97. 16) Al-Inshirah, 3. 17) Al-Isra, 29. 18) Al-Hijr, 72. 19) Al-Isra 29. 20) Al-Furqan, 32. 21) Al-Inshirah, 1.

Messages

1- The blessing of eloquence and its ease comes from Allah. "یسّرناه بلسانك"

2- The purpose of the Quran's revelation is to remind people. "یسّرناه... لعلهم یتذكّرون"

3- The teachings of the Quran conform to the innate nature (Fitrah). (Man inherently perceives truths, but forgets them due to certain factors, in which case he needs a reminder.) "یتذكّرون"

4- Allah has completed the argument (Itmam al-Hujjah) against people by sending the Quran; if they do not accept it, let them await Allah's wrath. "فارتقب انّهم مرتقبون"

5- Encourage your friends and threaten your enemies. "فارتقب انّهم مرتقبون"
